title: fixed json decoding bug in daemon thread
body: fixed bug in daemon thread that is triggered when trying to convert an empty message_json to dict. Still raising the JSONDecodeError in case decoding fails for any other reason

step_plan.summary: Fixes a daemon websocket receiver bug by skipping empty/whitespace messages before calling `json.loads()`, preventing thread crashes while still raising `JSONDecodeError` for non-empty malformed JSON.

steps.operation: Harden the websocket daemon receive loop to skip empty/whitespace payloads before JSON decoding, while preserving JSONDecodeError behavior for non-empty malformed messages
steps.reason: `json.loads('')` raises `JSONDecodeError`, which can crash the daemon receiver thread when `recv()` yields an empty/whitespace-only message. Guarding against empty payloads prevents the spurious failure without changing error semantics for genuinely invalid JSON.
steps.side_effects: Receiver thread will silently ignore empty/whitespace messages (optionally with debug logging if implemented), which may slightly change observability but should not affect protocol handling. Non-empty malformed JSON will still raise `JSONDecodeError`, so any callers/tests that rely on that exception behavior remain unchanged; verify thread lifecycle/error propagation remains acceptable under your existing exception handling.
